item.title: Traditional owners say connection to country ignored under flawed legislation
item.description: Traditional owners from WA's Wheatbelt say a historic native title settlement has increased division among Noongar groups.

item.title: NSW Police infiltrate 'VIP Network' underworld phones, send warning message
item.description: An encrypted phone network allegedly used by Sydney's underworld to orchestrate murders, kidnappings and drug deals has been infiltrated by NSW Police after they arrested a trio accused of running the "VIP Network".

item.title: Eight US men charged over alleged terror plot to kill Donald Trump at UFC White House event
item.description: Eight men have been accused of planning a drone and shooting attack that aimed to kill the US president and SpaceX founder at the UFC Freedom 250 event held at the White House in June.

item.title: Man sentenced to six years and five months jail over sex trafficking of Indonesian girl
item.description: A Sydney judge found 45-year-old Surya Subekti has demonstrated no genuine remorse.

item.title: Why potatoes can be the star of the show and what types to use
item.description: Potatoes are undeniably versatile and tasty but choosing which variety to buy has never been more complicated. Brushed or washed? Yellow, white or pink? Mashing or roasting? And now there are low-carb spuds too.

item.title: H5 bird flu strain detected in Australian seabird for first time
item.description: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.

item.title: Social media giants under fire for refusal to engage with antisemitism royal commission
item.description: Social media giants, including Elon Musk's X Corp, have come under fire with the royal commission on antisemitism expressing frustration at platforms' refusal to engage.

item.title: BBL to play first overseas match in India as Scorchers face Renegades
item.description: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

item.title: Police find human remains during search for missing Victorian teen
item.description: Detectives are investigating after human remains were found during a search for a missing teenage girl in Victoria's west.

item.title: Derryn Hinch, the 'human headline', former senator and broadcaster, dies aged 82
item.description: Hinch was known for his distinctive broadcast style, his time in the Senate, and his law-breaking crusades to expose sex offenders that earned him several stints in jail.

item.title: Claim woman's death caused by Telstra outage is 'incorrect', SA Police say
item.description: South Australia's police chief says there is "nothing to suggest" the death of a person in a regional hospital was caused by any failure to connect to Triple Zero (000) during the Telstra outage, following an investigation into the matter.

item.title: The Rolling Stones — Foreign Tongues review: a late-career peak in their eighties
item.description: On album #25, Foreign Tongues, rock's elder statesmen once again dismantle concerns whether men in their eighties can still make great records.

item.title: World Cup: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win in any fashion
item.description: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

item.title: Charlie Kirk's family requests evidence against accused killer be shown publicly
item.description: The family of conservative activist Charlie Kirk has pleaded with a judge to allow more public access to evidence presented against the man accused of the assassination.

item.title: Coffee Camp cattle farmer plants 14,000 trees for flood mitigation
item.description: Jeff Imeson has planted around 14,000 trees on his property near Nimbin in a bid to help slow the flow of future floods.

item.title: Australia's most played song on radio this week draws AI criticisms
item.description: A cover of the Madonna hit Like a Prayer is at the top of the charts but criticisms have been levied at the artist behind the cover, Josh Fawaz, over his suspected use of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Paul Okon-Engstler reportedly set to sign with German club FC Köln
item.description: Promising midfielder Paul Okon-Engstler is reportedly headed to Germany, the first of an emerging batch of Socceroos to move to bigger leagues.

item.title: Australian woman accused of illegally voting in US faces jury trial in Louisiana
item.description: A Sydney woman will face trial in the US state of Louisiana after being accused of falsely claiming to be a US citizen and voting illegally in two federal elections.

item.title: Lush native lawn ideas for waterwise, lower-maintenance gardens
item.description: If mowing the lawn is getting you down, it might be time to ditch your exotic buffalo, couch or kikuyu grass and switch to a native alternative.

item.title: Wallabies captain says recent results not good enough ahead of Test match against France
item.description: Close enough will not be good enough for the Wallabies, even with an untried flyhalf and familiar French pack ready to inflict a sixth-straight Test defeat on the hosts in Brisbane.

item.title: Scientists record spiders' speed and movement.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Professor Clemente explains insights from spider racing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tributes for Derryn Hinch after his death in Melbourne at the age of 82
item.description: Media identity Derryn Hinch is being remembered as a provocative but courageous journalist who was ahead of his time in his fight against institutional sex abuse.

item.title: Townsville apartment caretaker sentenced to nine years in jail for rape
item.description: Alexander Terry was sentenced to nine years in jail for the rape and sexual assault of four women at a Townsville apartment complex where he was a caretaker.

item.title: The ASX finished trading moderately higher on reports the US and Iran will meet for "technical talks".
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Pies coach says AFL review technology 'not up to scratch' after thriller
item.description: Craig McRae takes aim at the AFL's ARC after Collingwood's thrilling win over North Melbourne.

item.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket recovery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: China achieves first-ever controlled recovery of orbital-class rocket
item.description: China has successfully tested an experimental rocket retrieval system, state media reported, in the hope of breaking US dominance in reusable rockets.

item.title: 'Not gonna talk about that': Luai dodges press conference after another Tigers loss
item.description: The Tigers lose 32-6 to the Warriors at Campbelltown Stadium, with Jarome Luai skipping the post-match press conference after a week of reports about his future with the team.

item.title: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell in collision involving pedestrian
item.description: Police are investigating the collision which took place on Port Road at Woodville on Friday morning.

item.title: Bayeux Tapestry returns to Britain for first time in nearly 1,000 years
item.description: The Bayeux Tapestry has arrived in Britain for the first time in nearly 1,000 years, travelling under police escort during a meticulously choreographed journey from France.

item.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up new art gallery in Bangkok, Thailand
item.description: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

item.title: Cold Chisel saxophonist Andy Bickers dies from cancer, tributes paid to 'incredible musician'
item.description: Cold Chisel saxophone player Andy Bickers has died after "hard fought battle with cancer", the band has announced.

item.title: UK police arrest man, 26, over alleged murder of former minister Ann Widdecombe
item.description: The 78-year-old, who found fame as a Strictly Come Dancing contestant when she left office, was found dead at her home having sustained serious injuries.

item.title: Relentless Sinner downs Djokovic, Zverev ends Fery's dream run to set up blockbuster Wimbledon final
item.description: British wildcard Arthur Fery's bid to make Wimbledon history has ended after he was knocked out by Alexander Zverev, who will face off against reigning champion Jannik Sinner in the final.

item.title: Spain strikes late to seal semi berth after Belgium keeper howler
item.description: Spain will face France in a World Cup semifinal after beating Belgium 2-1 thanks to a late Mikel Merino goal and a goalkeeping error.

item.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Jurassic Park, the Scottish play and Wimbledon fruit
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: All-star cast set sail in Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey
item.description: Christopher Nolan and the cast of the highly anticipated The Odyssey sit down with ABC News Breakfast to talk about the upcoming film.

item.title: Cape Fear TV show, starring Amy Adams and Javier Bardem, has distinctly modern menace
item.description: The 1957 book The Executioners has been made into two movies and an iconic episode of The Simpsons. It's now a TV show, starring Amy Adams, Patrick Wilson and Javier Bardem.

item.title: Telstra faces outage investigation as expert says legal gaps may let it off
item.description: Telstra faces an outage investigation as an expert says legal gaps may let the telco off the hook.

item.title: The battle for the Blue Zones
item.description: The Blue Zones grew into a global wellness brand aided by stories of legendary old age. But a bitter feud over the science has shaken their foundations.

item.title: BYD backflips and offers full refunds after customers given cars from wrong year
item.description: More than 1,200 customers who bought electric and hybrid cars from Chinese maker BYD will be offered a full refund after the company admitted to selling them vehicles from the wrong year.

item.title: He wasn't perfect, but Derryn Hinch confronted sexual abuse when others turned a blind eye
item.description: Derryn Hinch was not a hero to every survivor of sexual abuse, but in the decades when they lacked allies, he was their fiercest public advocate.

item.title: NATO was a lesson in how the geopolitical landscape has shifted around Trump
item.description: Trump demanded to be the centre of attention. But the more significant news out of NATO was really about everyone else.

item.title: Curbing well-off taxpayers' access to key programs could save billions
item.description: Curbing access to the child care subsidy, parental leave pay, aged care and pensions for some of the most well-off taxpayers would free up about $21 billion a year, a new report says.

item.title: The Interview: Jessica Mauboy wants to share the culture and community of her home
item.description: From singing to her daughter to laughing with her aunties, Jessica Mauboy is all about family. This NAIDOC Week, the star is sharing the music, culture and community she grew up with in a whole new way.

item.title: The World Cup just had one of its hottest matches, in a challenge for the sport
item.description: A 38-degree Celsius evening match has raised fresh questions about how football manages heat in a warming world.

item.title: NT Police release new footage of Bradley John Murdoch ahead of 25th anniversary of Falconio mystery
item.description: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of British tourist Peter Falconio's remains.

item.title: Handmade quilts weave culture into Forster NAIDOC Week baby celebration
item.description: Beautiful Indigenous-themed quilts have been made by a group of women for a NAIDOC Week baby celebration on Worimi country, linking cultures and generations.

item.title: Cruz Hewitt becomes first Australian since Alex de Minaur to reach Wimbledon boys' final
item.description: Cruz Hewitt continues his dominant run at Wimbledon, keeping his straight sets record intact en-route to the boys' final.

item.title: What antisemitism inquiry revealed about social media giants and online 'Jew hatred'
item.description: It was gloves off at the royal commission on antisemitism this week, with Elon Musk's X bearing the brunt of the inquiry's frustrations with obstructive social media giants.

item.title: H5 bird flu poses risk to SA's Coorong critical wetlands
item.description: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

item.title: Fears workers will never receive entitlement from GFG's liquidated companies
item.description: Jun Samonte has spent 14 years working for the same Whyalla business. He now fears he will never receive the $53,000 he is owed in entitlements, despite already waiting a year.

item.title: Former Sydney high school students lead blockbuster movies Moana and Supergirl
item.description: Some cities have one hometown hero, but Newtown has two making the community proud in a double-feature of blockbuster hits you could watch on the same day.

item.title: Aboriginal artist combining centuries-old glassblowing technique with millennia-old traditional weaving practice
item.description: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Spain's new clutch king and a Colombian death threat scare
item.description: Spain's new clutch king saves the day, a record-breaking streak is finally broken and a Colombian midfielder receives death threats. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Living in the 'forgotten' community of Beagle Bay
item.description: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

item.title: Broken Hill mother exposed to lead as a child fights same battle with her children
item.description: Baylee Stokes lives with the health impacts of being exposed to lead as a child. Now, she's considering moving away from her hometown to protect her own children.

item.title: Derryn Hinch's memorable media moments: from radio wars with prime ministers to Dancing with the Stars
item.description: A man of colourful contradictions, Derryn Hinch was a larger-than-life fixture on Australian radio and television over five decades who died on Friday morning at the age of 82.

item.title: Polling shows Labor is still sliding in Tasmania, despite facing a 12-year-old minority government
item.description: When Labor recorded its lowest electoral result in over a century last year some may have felt the party had hit rock bottom, at least in terms of polling. That may not be entirely the case, writes Lucy MacDonald.

item.title: Passenger hospitalised after being partially sucked out of window on Ryanair flight from Greece
item.description: The man's head and shoulders were reportedly outside the Boeing 737's window before passengers on the Ryanair flight were able to pull him back in.

item.title: Remembering the quadriplegic artist who drew the outback without using his hands
item.description: Left quadriplegic in an accident, every line the late Gunggari man drew and every word he scrawled in ink was created without a pen in his hands.

item.title: A warming veggie and lentil soup that's ready in less than 30 minutes - ABC News
item.description: This easy, healthy, pantry-based soup is a saviour if your fridge is looking bare.

item.title: Hepburn Shire mayor says charges in 'private prosecution' by resident a 'threat to democracy'
item.description: The mayor of Hepburn Shire in Victoria is the third council representative to be charged by the same resident via a rare "private prosecution".

item.title: Details emerge about 16 children who lived in Ohio house of horrors
item.description: From moving around southern Ohio without leaving a paper trail, to being married when they were teenagers, details are emerging about the Siders family.

item.title: World Cup: England captain Harry Kane confirms 'surreal' golf date with US President Donald Trump
item.description: England's captain Harry Kane opens up about his 18-hole golf experience with the US President on a previous visit to Palm Beach, Florida.

item.title: Carbon monoxide poisoning suspected in fatal Mount Gambier incident
item.description: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

item.title: Hardwick lashes 'peanut' football fans after fiery run-in with spectator
item.description: Damien Hardwick clashes with Crows fans at Adelaide Oval, as Jeremy Cameron's injury timeline is revealed.

item.title: Jarome Luai to leave Wests Tigers at end of season
item.description: Wests Tigers have confirmed star five-eighth Jarome Luai will leave the club at the end of the season.

item.title: Meta pulls new Instagram AI feature after widespread backlash
item.description: The automatic feature, integrated into its Meta AI chatbot, let users edit generated images directly through sketches.

item.title: Bail refused for man accused of damaging EV charging stations across the Central Coast and Newcastle
item.description: Dylan Hyland, 29, is facing 26 charges relating to the theft of copper cables from EV chargers in the NSW Central Coast and Newcastle regions.

item.title: World Cup: England and Norway fans make late dash to Miami for football's spring break
item.description: As England and Norway prepare to face off in their World Cup quarter-final, their fans are lapping up being in one of the party capitals of the world, making every moment count.

item.title: 'Lost feeling down one arm': Dogs coach reveals extent of Crichton injury
item.description: Canterbury captain Stephen Crichton is removed from the the Bulldogs' 40-16 loss to the Raiders on a medicab, with coach Cameron Ciraldo confirming a recurrence of nerve issues in his neck and shoulder.

item.title: Telstra will face Senate inquiry after nationwide outage
item.description: The telco will face a grilling from politicians about this week's nationwide outage that brought businesses and transport systems into chaos.

item.title: France dominates Wallabies to post historic Test win
item.description: Following a second-half blitz, France scores its fourth straight victory over the Wallabies, triumphing 42-26 in the Nations Championship Test in Brisbane.

item.title: Suspect in former UK MP Ann Widdecombe's murder investigation released
item.description: UK police say the man has been released from custody and is "no longer part of the investigation" into Ann Widdecombe's death.

item.title: Speedboat capsize off Vietnam's Phu Quoc island kills 15 tourists
item.description: A tourist boat returning from an island trip in southern Vietnam has capsized, killing 15 Indian visitors, state media has reported.

item.title: Devon, polony, fritz: The food that goes by a half dozen different names
item.description: The words we use for a handful of classic Australian foods can tell a lot about where we come from.

item.title: The Invite welcomes you to one of the best films of 2026
item.description: Olivia Wilde's return to directing might take place almost entirely in a single room, but it unfolds all the relationship anxieties we prefer to keep hidden at home.

item.title: Australia's first map of city zoning shows Melbourne streets ahead on density
item.description: Four years on from a national pledge to unlock more housing, only one capital city allows townhouses or three-storey developments on most of its residential land.

item.title: Innovative program sparks unexpected friendships between Jewish volunteers and Muslim refugees
item.description: When a group of Jewish mates decided to support a newly arrived Muslim family, all of their lives would change. Together they have flipped expectations about religion and friendship.

item.title: Can Australia avoid the next round of the global energy crisis?
item.description: As the conflict has dragged on, gas prices have soared and now threaten to up-end the economies of Europe and Asia.

item.title: As Japan's population shrinks, bears are moving close to town and creating a big problem
item.description: When Araki Osamu first heard reports a bear had been seen in the streets of his home town, a 1-hour bullet train ride north of Tokyo, he dismissed it as "fake news".

item.title: Israeli court battle threatens to turn into constitutional crisis
item.description: A Trumpian clash between the Netanyahu government and Israel's most senior court is rapidly building into a major constitutional crisis.

item.title: Telstra outage fallout will put overseas holidays on the backburner for anxious leaders
item.description: For Australian politicians, Scott Morrison's ill-fated Hawaii vacation in December 2019 remains a potent reminder of what not to do in a national crisis.

item.title: Grace Woodroofe on Heath Ledger, healing and turning abuse into art for new album
item.description: Heath Ledger launched her music career at just 17, but an emotionally abusive relationship with a fellow musician derailed it. A decade on, Grace Woodroofe is releasing the soundtrack to her survival.

item.title: Rare glimpse into Discovery expedition helps scientist better understand the present
item.description: Hidden for more than a century, a remarkable collection is offering a rare glimpse into the famed 1900s Discovery expedition to Antarctica.

item.title: House prices are falling but that doesn't necessarily mean they're affordable yet
item.description: Australian house prices have recorded their biggest monthly fall since 2022. For young buyers hoping it's a turning point, economists have some bad news.

item.title: Linda Nosková survives Karolína Muchová fightback in all-Czech final to lift Wimbledon women's singles trophy
item.description: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.

item.title: Álvarez wondergoal helps Argentina book semifinal date against England
item.description: Julián Álvarez's first World Cup goal breaks the deadlock as Argentina downs Switzerland 3-1 to advance to the semifinals.

item.title: South African World Cup player Jayden Adams dies aged 25
item.description: Jayden Adams, who made three appearances for South Africa at the World Cup, has died aged 25.

item.title: Socceroos star Jordy Bos sidelined for months after undergoing successful knee surgery
item.description: Jordy Bos goes under the knife after his World Cup campaign came to an abrupt end due to a knee injury against Egypt.

item.title: Fake mountain, MONA-style hub and Aboriginal gallery among old RAH plans that never materialised
item.description: Adelaide's stalled Aboriginal cultural centre has a dubious future on a site with a troubled past. The Malinauskas government faces criticism whatever it chooses to do, writes state political reporter Thomas Kelsall.

item.title: Invisible string that Australia attached to PNG's rugby league team
item.description: In a contest for Pacific influence against China, Australia's football diplomacy comes with risks and rewards.

item.title: Man charged with murder after body found in search for missing mother
item.description: A 48-year-old man has been charged with murder after the body of missing mother Jana Armstrong was found in bushland late on Saturday.

item.title: Online radio stations rise in popularity as Sydney creatives turn to new platforms
item.description: Unlike 'terrestrial' stations, which transmit over AM or FM airwaves via radio towers, online radio is a more DIY approach.

item.title: Historic Brickendon Estate turns traditional Sunday roast into paddock-to-plate agritourism experience
item.description: A 200-year-old Tasmanian farm is opening its gates to diners for the first time, as the family-run business seeks to expand its offerings and keep on top of costly upkeep.

item.title: Ensay's football homecoming revives decades-old legacy
item.description: A special tribute match at Ensay's oval in regional Victoria resurrected a football tradition and brought generations of a Gippsland high country community together.

item.title: Russian glide bombs strike Sumy in Ukraine
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Cost-conscious Canberra cyclists continue bracing against winter's chill
item.description: Every year, winter sees many Canberrans hanging up their helmets, despite the city being considered Australia's "cycling capital." This year though, cyclist numbers have remained relatively steady, even as the mercury drops.

item.title: Artist Julie Aldridge's 10-year battle to reclaim mosaic design from the internet
item.description: A South Australian artist is fighting drop-ship company Temu over its selling of her mosaic design on rugs. But how did the artwork get there in the first place?

item.title: NT government faces Federal Court challenge over laws capping compensation payouts
item.description: The NT government's controversial laws capping compensation for prisoners unlawfully assaulted, strip searched or imprisoned are being challenged in the Federal Court.

item.title: How a fireman helped make dinosaur fossils cool again
item.description: Travis Enright is four months into investigating what he calls "a 100-million-year-old cold case" involving a 10-metre-long aquatic dinosaur called Moria.

item.title: Six teenagers charged after armed robberies across Melbourne
item.description: Police tracked an allegedly stolen car across multiple Melbourne suburbs before making dramatic arrests.

item.title: Wajarri man Kurt McIntosh picked up a book at an airport. It transformed his life
item.description: Board meetings and navigating native title were not on Kurt McIntosh's radar until he stumbled upon a book at the airport.

item.title: What the Australia-India uranium deal could mean for Western Australia
item.description: Australia has struck a uranium exports deal with India, which has put WA’s effective ban on uranium mining and exporting in the spotlight.

item.title: Horsham footballer 'alert' in Melbourne hospital after on-field head knock
item.description: A young regional footballer who sustained a head knock and concussion during a match on Saturday is recovering well in a Melbourne hospital, according to his club.

item.title: Numbat conservation efforts pull native species from brink of extinction
item.description: Numbats, Western Australia's animal emblem, have been pulled from the brink of extinction after research found the population was growing in the wild.

item.title: Junior Perth doctor, 26, charged over death near Fremantle's entertainment precinct
item.description: A junior doctor from Perth's western suburbs appears in court charged with manslaughter following the death of a 63-year-old man near Fremantle's entertainment strip in the early hours yesterday.

item.title: China ballistic missile test pushes forward case for Pacific Security Agreement
item.description: Pacific Minister Pat Conroy says the testing of a nuclear-capable missile damaged China's reputation and has strengthened the case for a Pacific security pact.

item.title: Love story: Motherhood is helping Moana Hope re-learn how to love
item.description: The former AFLW player was an adult when she realised her childhood had warped her understanding of love.

item.title: World Cup controversy as Norway fumes over England's spidercam-assisted equaliser
item.description: Norway felt England's equaliser shouldn't have stood after the ball appeared to get a deflection off a wire holding up the "spidercam" above the pitch, directly leading to England gaining possession and scoring.

item.title: Newcastle unit block searched by police after man fatally stabbed
item.description: An extensive forensic examination is underway at a unit block in Hamilton South after a man was fatally stabbed.

item.title: World Cup: Bellingham goes big time again, as England continues down hard road
item.description: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

item.title: 'Can't knock Zach': Dons coach defends Merrett despite horror showing
item.description: The Brisbane Lions have taken a big win over Essendon to snatch a top-four spot after round 18, as Melbourne and the Western Bulldogs enjoy their respective victories. Follow live.

item.title: Souths win on Arrow's birthday, Cowboys and Storm snatch late victories
item.description: South Sydney captain Cam Murray praised his side for being able to focus on the field as the league celebrated the "world's biggest birthday party" for Jai Arrow.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Breel Embolo's red, Lionel Messi's perfect 10, Norway and England's VAR frustrations
item.description: Norway fumes at England's equaliser, Breel Embolo's tearful red card and Jordan Henderson's heavy-duty cast. Here are today's quick hits from the World Cup.

item.title: US launches strikes on Iranian targets
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US strikes Iranian targets with precision munitions
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Can the pill affect your mental health? Young woman says mood swings were too much to bear
item.description: Two young women say taking the pill to manage painful periods had side effects they weren't expecting.

item.title: World Cup: Switzerland blasts 'unacceptable' VAR rule after 3-1 loss to Argentina
item.description: Switzerland is fuming after Breel Embolo was sent of the highly charged World Cup quarterfinal against Argentina in Kansas City.

item.title: Jai Arrow vows to fight MND for everyone during 'World's Biggest Birthday Party'
item.description: Former NRL player Jai Arrow says he is determined to raise money and awareness for Motor Neurone Disease until a cure is found.

item.title: Lindsey Graham, pivotal US Republican senator, dies suddenly at 71
item.description: US senator Lindsey Graham, a key Republican who went from a vocal critic of Donald Trump to one of his most loyal allies, has died at age 71.

item.title: Victim of antisemitic attack wants graffiti to remain to show family was targeted
item.description: The victim of an antisemitic attack says he doesn't want to clean up offensive graffiti sprayed on his Adelaide property, because he wants the public to see his family was targeted

item.title: Family of Perth man Cameron Hughes raises questions about his Bali detention centre death
item.description: The family of Perth man Cameron Hughes, who died in a Bali immigration detention centre, says it has many questions about the circumstances surrounding his death.

item.title: Man, 45, wanted over alleged attempted murder of 74yo Yennora man
item.description: An arrest warrant has been issued for a 45-year-old man in connection to a violent assault in Western Sydney on Saturday.

item.title: Pharmacy lobby preventing Australians from buying cheaper medicines, Grattan Institute says
item.description: Taxpayers are missing out on cheaper medicines due to backroom deals by one of Australia's most powerful lobby groups, according to a new report.

item.title: Universities required to define antisemitism under new racism standards
item.description: The new standards will require institutions to adopt definitions of antisemitism, racism against Aboriginal and Torres Straight Islander peoples, and Islamophobia.

item.title: This week in history quiz, July 13: Test your recall on Taylor Swift, the Tour de France and the Salem witch trials
item.description: How much do you know about long-past British monarchs, the Salem witch trials and Nintendo games of the 80s? Find out with this week's quiz.

item.title: Small and achievable ways to drink less alcohol
item.description: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

item.title: Four Corners analysed 'affordable' rental listings. We found a reality that didn't match the promise
item.description: Governments across Australia are spending billions to boost the number of rentals available to people on low and middle incomes. A Four Corners investigation found many of these homes were unaffordable for those who needed them most, particularly single-income households.

item.title: Australian Islamic State-linked woman was enforcer of Sharia law in Syrian camp, ABC told
item.description: Speaking exclusively to the ABC, a security source who dealt directly with Australian woman Hodan Abby calls her a driving force behind many of a Syrian detention camp's problems.

item.title: Jewish staff and students speak out as antisemitism royal commission shifts focus to universities
item.description: Jewish academics and students say they do not feel safe on campuses, as the antisemitism royal commission prepares to hold a week of hearings scrutinising universities.

item.title: With AI, we have returned to a world of two competing superpowers
item.description: When it comes to AI, Australia is a spectator at a UFC cage fight between the United States and China, except the spectators are inside the cage with the combatants.

item.title: Labor's trust tax proposal leaves small businesses waiting to see what states will do with stamp duty
item.description: Small businesses affected by the change are in limbo as states decide whether to waive stamp duty fees, with industry groups warning relief will be limited unless they sign on.

item.title: Digital arrival cards for international travellers to roll out more widely
item.description: A digital alternative to incoming passenger cards filled out by everyone entering Australia is to be implemented in the next two years.

item.title: Labor scoring own goals while Coalition distracted by One Nation threat
item.description: When the bells resume ringing in the halls of parliament, Anthony Albanese and Angus Taylor will both do well to dispose of any complacency.

item.title: Swift parrots in Lonnavale forest.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: As swift parrots kept breeding in this forest, the government realised it had a problem
item.description: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

item.title: Report says growing Sydney's tree canopy would save $1 billion in health costs
item.description: A series of greenways crisscrossing Sydney's west presents an opportunity to grow the city's canopy and make more public space, a Committee for Sydney report suggests.

item.title: 'Hugely successful' Queensland firefighter camp for girls scrapped after one year despite low diversity rates
item.description: The free camp ran over a weekend in June 2024 for students in years 9 and 10, exploring firefighting activities, road crashes, swiftwater rescues, and physical fitness training.

item.title: Jannik Sinner beats Alexander Zverev to win Wimbledon again
item.description: The top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Alexander Zverev for his second consecutive title at Wimbledon after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue.

item.title: SA doctor banned for inappropriate relationship with patient who accused him of 'grooming' her
item.description: A former Adelaide doctor will not be able to reapply for registration for at least two years after he was banned for having an inappropriate relationship with his client.

item.title: Bangkok pub fire kills at least 27 people and injures 63 others
item.description: A huge fire engulfed a pub in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people before firefighters brought the blaze under control.

item.title: Tour de Cure reflects on Richard Scolyer's enduring legacy ahead of state memorial service
item.description: As Mark Beretta rode his bike up a steep Tasmanian mountain in March with all his might, it was none other than Richard Scolyer who overtook him.

item.title: Homicide squad arrests man after dead body found in Morwell home
item.description: Police arrest a 43-year-old man after a dead body was found in a home east of Melbourne.

item.title: Noosa Council and Queensland Police crack down on illegal camping
item.description: A crackdown on illegal camping in Noosa, prompted by hundreds of complaints, has sparked outrage locally, with many arguing fines will not solve the issue.

item.title: 'Like my men's shed': John O'Toole celebrates over 600 matches with Huon Valley Soccer Club
item.description: For more than 30 years, John O'Toole has quietly helped build a local soccer club. On Sunday, he surpassed his 600th match while playing alongside his sons.

item.title: Convicted paedophile argues multiple-personality evidence unlawful in appeal
item.description: A father jailed for abusing two children is appealing against his conviction, arguing evidence given by the multiple personalities of the same witness was unlawful.

item.title: Brendon McCullum fired as England's Test coach but retains white ball duties
item.description: The architect of England's controversial Bazball approach to Test cricket is given his marching orders after a disappointing string of results.

item.title: ASX flat as oil price jumps, Telstra warns of post-outage scams — as it happened
item.description: The ASX has closed the session flat despite renewed war tensions, while Telstra warned customers about a spate of postage scamming.

item.title: Burke says it 'beggars belief' how IS-linked woman 'treated other humans' — as it happened
item.description: The home affairs minister says he will "not lift a finger" to support suspected Australian Islamic State fighters held in an Iraqi prison.

item.title: Why the world's biggest AI companies want data centres in the NT
item.description: The NT's sunny skies, natural gas reserves and proximity to Asia have caught the attention of a dozen data centre developers.

item.title: Man's death 'not suspicious' after 'large fight' on APY Lands, police say
item.description: Police said the death of a 59-year-old man was not suspicious after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, but investigations into the disturbance are continuing.

item.title: Cruz Hewitt beaten by American prospect Jordan Lee in Wimbledon boys' final
item.description: Cruz Hewitt comes up short in his bid to win the Wimbledon boys' title, being beaten by Jordan Lee in the final.

item.title: Once-booming gold rush town calls for government investment ahead of World Heritage Bid
item.description: Once a booming gold rush town, Walhalla is preparing for a different kind of boom, but first, it needs critical government investment in infrastructure.

item.title: Coles Bay man recovering in hospital after home invaders tie him up and burn down his house
item.description: A victim of an early morning robbery was hogtied and had to watch for hours as his home burnt to the ground after two home invaders robbed him and then set fire to his property, his family says.

item.title: NDIS cuts threaten blind bowler's dream of representing Australia
item.description: A blind bowler from regional WA say his dreams of representing Australia are being swept away along with his NDIS funding.

item.title: AFLW star Zarlie Goldsworthy on that viral social media clip and the tough years at GWS
item.description: AFLW star Zarlie Goldsworthy used to carry the losses as GWS languished down the bottom of the ladder. She's learnt to let them go and stay motived.

item.title: US announces new strikes against Iran after attacks reported in Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: Fresh attacks have rocked Iran and Kuwait as Tehran and Washington square off over the Strait of Hormuz, offering contradicting statements as to whether the vital waterway was open to traffic.

item.title: North Midlands annual golf day rallies support for first aid
item.description: The long wait for an ambulance in WA's Wheatbelt makes first aid critical, and these golfers are ensuring their community is well prepared.

item.title: Slow Read exhibition asks us to ditch doom-scrolling for art celebrating the printed page
item.description: A new show at Hawthorn Arts Centre offers a range of immersive artworks made from books and paper that demand, and reward, our full attention.

item.title: Proposed amendment to Arapiles Dyurrite plan allows climbing near culturally significant areas
item.description: Climbing Victoria says proposed changes to the Mount Arapiles Dyurrite management plan will be a compromise between climbers and traditional owners built on "trust".

item.title: Gianni Infantino says FIFA will discuss World Cup expansion post-tournament, defends hydration breaks
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino believes this year's World Cup is proof that expanding the tournament was the right move.

item.title: 'He continues to inspire us': Richard Scolyer's family pay emotional tribute
item.description: Richard Scolyer, a pioneer of cancer research, has been remembered for his "truly remarkable life" in a state memorial service at the Sydney Opera House.

item.title: Toowoomba man Dharminder Singh faces court charged with the murder of Jana Armstrong
item.description: Toowoomba man Dharminder Singh, 48, whose former partner, Jana Armstrong's body was discovered on the weekend, has appeared in a Darling Downs court charged with murder.

item.title: Death toll rises as millions stranded by Bangladesh floods
item.description: Days of torrential monsoon rain have triggered major flooding, leaving millions stranded and facing food supply shortages in the south-east of the country.

item.title: Min Woo Lee finishes runner-up at Scottish Open as Tom Kim takes out title
item.description: Min Woo Lee comes close to emulating his feats from 2021 but is denied by a bogey-free run-in from eventual winner Tom Kim.

item.title: Queensland teen dies, 22 injured in e-mobility device crashes since safety laws brought in
item.description: Regulations introduced in Queensland less than a fortnight ago limit e-mobility devices to 12km/h on footpaths. Riders must be 16 and above or be supervised by a parent. They can also be breathalysed.

item.title: Consumer advocates intensify calls for mandatory domestic mobile roaming
item.description: Already available to international tourists, consumer advocates want to see mobile roaming mandated for domestic customers too.

item.title: Chris Minns pays tribute to Richard Scolyer
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Family of Melbourne woman Lavanya Chappa pay tribute to 'loving mother'
item.description: Lavanya Chappa has been remembered as a devoted mother and dear friend, one week after the mother-of-two was stabbed to death in her Melbourne home.

item.title: Drone vision shows swollen river flooding Bangladesh village
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How Lindsey Graham's death could affect Donald Trump's agenda
item.description: US senator Lindsey Graham died suddenly on Saturday night, local time, creating an unexpected vacancy in Congress. Here's what we know about what comes next.

item.title: Letters from cancer survivors read out thanking Richard Scolyer
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Israelis will vote in October, as polling suggests Netanyahu faces battle to secure power
item.description: Israelis will head to the polls on the last Tuesday in October for an election that will serve as a referendum on the legacy of the country's longest-serving prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: The main culprit of Australia's productivity crisis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: As new strikes rattle the Gulf, Iran's lead negotiator sends a clear message to Washington
item.description: As another round of strikes rattles the Gulf region, Iran's lead negotiator makes it very clear where the regime stands on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Sentencing of Molly Ticehurst's killer Daniel Billings brought forward
item.description: The sentencing of the man who murdered New South Wales woman Molly Ticehurst has been brought forward to allow a psychiatrist to give evidence.

item.title: Former National Trust of Tasmania director Scott Carlin unfairly dismissed, Fair Work Commission finds
item.description: The Fair Work Commission has found Scott Carlin, former managing director of the National Trust of Tasmania, was unfairly dismissed just two months before his two-year contract was set to expire.

item.title: Bazball is over as Brendon McCullum's deliberately uncomplicated regime falls foul of itself, leaving complicated legacy
item.description: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.

item.title: Jewish Australian university staff and students say they felt unsafe on campus
item.description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion has heard universities need to improve responses when Jewish staff and students feel unsafe.

item.title: Thomas Tuchel's exchange with Jude Bellingham gives England another chance to prove its World Cup mettle
item.description: This England World Cup squad is proving they are different to their predecessors at every stop. Now their manager has just given them their latest challenge.

item.title: Teen in custody after allegedly stabbing 15-year-old at Cairns high school
item.description: A teenage boy has been arrested over an alleged stabbing at a high school in Cairns.

item.title: Payne Haas to stay with Samoa and says nation is 'big chance' to win Rugby League World Cup
item.description: Payne Haas has cleared the air about his World Cup plans and says there is a "great chance" Samoa can lift the trophy for the first time.

item.title: Queensland government defends $40m cut to family violence prevention funding
item.description: The Queensland opposition has labelled the cuts to family and domestic violence funding "indefensible".

item.title: Five of our best chicken dinner recipes
item.description: From pot pie to tray bakes and sticky maple wings, here are five of our most popular chicken recipes.

item.title: Greyhound industry reaches compliance deadline after animal abuse revelations
item.description: A final report on greyhound industry compliance with improved standards following revelations of widespread abuse is expected to be handed to the SA government in coming months.

item.title: Sea World Helicopters CEO tells inquest crash could have been avoided if pilot looked right
item.description: The chief executive of Sea World Helicopters told a Brisbane inquest he believed pilot Ashley Jenkinson was "totally distracted" by the front row passengers on the joy flight.

item.title: Actor Sam Neill, star of Jurassic Park, dies
item.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, who starred in My Brilliant Career and Jurassic Park, has died aged 78.

item.title: Man dies after dog attack on Coonabarabran property
item.description: The 55-year-old died at the scene. Police officers euthanased the animal.

item.title: Rescuers retrieve a sick seabird from Noosa Main Beach
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland wildlife rescuers prepare for bird flu after sick giant petrel found at Noosa
item.description: A northern giant petrel found at Noosa Main Beach is undergoing testing for H5 avian influenza.

item.title: Actor Sam Neill dies aged 78
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: SA coroner calls for changes after man died while paramedic waited outside
item.description: A paramedic who chose not to force entry to a house, despite being told a man was inside and needed medical attention, had "a flawed understanding" of his powers to enter the property, an inquest finds.

item.title: Sam Neill says he loves acting, but he also relishes being back among his winemaking community.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Actor Sam Neill's final take
item.description: Actor Sam Neill might have officially been a Kiwi but he did some of his best work in Australian film and television. At home on either side of the ditch, this is Sam Neill's story.

item.title: A-League men's and women's seasons uncertain after players reject pay deal
item.description: A-Leagues' athletes and the competition's bosses are at loggerheads over a new pay deal after players voted against the latest proposal.

item.title: SA Jewish community condemns antisemitic attack on Adelaide home
item.description: The Jewish Community Council of South Australia condemns an act of vandalism committed on a man's Adelaide home as police continue to investigate.

item.title: What we know about Christian Jack Joseph, the Perth doctor accused of killing a man in Fremantle
item.description: A young Perth doctor and musician is behind bars, accused of killing a 63-year-old man after a late-night Fremantle scuffle. What do we know?
